title: West Asia War Nears End: Where Do the US, Iran, and Israel Stand on June 17?
summary: A comprehensive status report as of June 15 shows the US and Iran set to sign a peace deal in Switzerland on June 19, with the Strait of Hormuz blockade — in place since February — set to lift. Israel, however, retains positions in Lebanon and Gaza on a separate, unresolved negotiating track, meaning the broader West Asia conflict is winding down unevenly rather than ending in a single clean resolution.

coverages.headline: West Asia war nears end: Where do the US, Iran, Israel stand today?
coverages.excerpt: The US and Iran are set to sign a peace deal in Switzerland on June 19 and the Strait of Hormuz blockade — in place since February — will be lifted; Israel retains positions in Lebanon and Gaza with a separate negotiating track.
coverages.bias_notes: This status-report format is useful precisely because it resists the urge to declare the conflict definitively over. The key distinction the piece draws — US-Iran de-escalation versus Israel's separate and unresolved tracks in Lebanon and Gaza — is the detail that gets flattened in coverage that talks about 'the war ending' as a single event. For India, the Hormuz reopening is the most economically consequential piece of this story, but the continued Israeli positions in Lebanon mean West Asia is not returning to a pre-crisis baseline; shipping, insurance, and diplomatic risk calculations should reflect a partial, not complete, normalisation.
